title: NPC Physical Traits
description: Distinctive physical details for an NPC — the feature that makes them visually identifiable at a glance. Roll once for a quick impression or several times to build a fuller picture.

entries.result: Extremely tall, with a posture that suggests they've spent years trying to fit through doorways.
Missing the top joint of the index finger on their right hand.
Hair completely white despite an otherwise young face.
A scar running from the corner of the mouth toward the ear, thin and old.
Eyes of two different colors — one dark, one pale.
Moves with the careful, deliberate stillness of someone who learned not to draw attention.
Extraordinarily large hands for their frame.
A significant burn scar covering much of the left forearm, faded to silver-pink.
Walks with a slight limp in the right leg that they clearly work to conceal.
A broken nose, set imperfectly, giving the face a slightly asymmetrical quality.
Extremely thin, in a way that suggests constitution rather than poverty.
Short and powerfully built, with the kind of muscular density that doesn't announce itself.
A prominent birthmark on the left side of the neck, dark and roughly the size of a thumb.
Fingers stained permanently at the tips from long work with pigments or chemicals.
Noticeably long neck, giving the head an elevated, slightly regal quality.
One ear significantly higher than the other, which the face is shaped to make less noticeable than it is.
A face that reads as much older than the rest of the body.
A full beard that they keep extremely well-maintained, contrasting with otherwise plain dress.
Hair worn in an elaborate style that takes obvious time and communicates that they have it.
An old surgical scar visible above their collar, clean and professional.
Calluses on both hands concentrated at specific points that identify their trade to anyone who looks.
Eyebrows so pale they're nearly invisible, giving the upper face a blank look in certain light.
A prosthetic hand, functional, well-maintained, worn openly.
A broad, flat nose that dominates the face and gives expressions a compressed quality.
Moves very quickly — not rushedly, just efficiently, as though they're always running slightly late.
Close-cropped hair with a prominent scar on the scalp visible beneath it.
Teeth notably well-maintained for someone of apparent means or station.
Teeth notably poor, which they're self-conscious about in a way that slightly changes how they speak.
Eyes set unusually deep, giving them a watching quality.
A tattoo on the back of the neck, old and slightly faded.
Heavy bags under the eyes regardless of apparent rest — structural, not situational.
Moves with the measured economy of someone with a chronic pain they manage.
A broad, flat face with features that seem compressed horizontally.
One arm clearly stronger than the other in ways clothing can't fully hide.
A pronounced jaw that makes the face read as determined from below and severe from above.
Hair that stands up naturally and has clearly defeated every attempt to make it do otherwise.
Very pale skin with veins visible at the temples and wrists.
Dark skin with a visible patchwork of lighter patches from old sun exposure or scarring.
A scar through one eyebrow, breaking it into two distinct halves.
Unusually narrow shoulders for their height.
A voice significantly deeper than their appearance would suggest.
A voice significantly higher than their appearance would suggest.
Fingernails bitten to the quick.
Fingernails kept long and clean in a way that implies they don't do rough work.
A distinctive walk — not a limp, but a particular rhythm, like a march that never fully stopped.
Ink stains on the dominant hand that never fully wash out.
A single streak of a different color running through otherwise dark hair, natural or chosen.
Visible scarring from a burn that covers part of the face, faded but present.
A face with absolutely no apparent age — could be thirty or sixty.
A face with apparent age that seems slightly more than their energy level would suggest.
Always has a small amount of residue from their work — flour, sawdust, ash — somewhere on their person.
Ears that stick out slightly, which they seem aware of and have made peace with.
A nose that was clearly larger before whatever event reshaped it.
Eyebrows so expressive that they communicate full sentences before the mouth opens.
Eyes that remain still when speaking — they listen with their whole face but their eyes don't move much.
A very slight tremor in one hand that appears only when they're not concentrating on suppressing it.
Deep-set laugh lines around the eyes and mouth, regardless of current expression.
A forehead scar from something blunt, healed but visible, running horizontally.
A neck scar that they don't hide but don't explain.
Very thick hair worn simply in a way that suggests they stopped fighting it long ago.
Missing an earlobe on the left ear — clean, old wound.
A dimple on only one side of the face, which makes their smile look slightly asymmetric.
Stands with their weight slightly forward, as though perpetually about to move.
Stands with their weight back, arms loose, in a way that reads as watchful relaxation.
A pronounced widow's peak that makes the hairline dramatic even with short hair.
Completely bald, with a head shape that makes it look intentional rather than inevitable.
Thinning hair they've styled to acknowledge rather than disguise.
A single prominent gold or silver tooth.
Slightly crossed eyes, mild enough that it's noticeable only in certain light.
A scar across the palm of one hand that they gesture with constantly.
Very long fingers, pianist's hands on someone who has never played.
Short, wide fingers well-suited to work and displaying the evidence of it.
An unusual gait — very light-footed for their size, feet barely audible on any surface.
An unusual gait — heavier than expected, each footfall deliberate.
High cheekbones that give the face a sculpted, slightly formal look.
A round face that tends to read as friendly regardless of expression.
A long face that reads as serious regardless of expression.
Several small scars on the knuckles of both hands.
A small cluster of freckles on one cheek that ends with unusual abruptness.
A small scar below one eye — round, like a coin.
Very dark eyes that catch light in an unsettling way.
Very pale eyes that seem to register more than they let on.
A habitually tilted head that gives conversations a canine, attentive quality.
An extremely straight spine and squared shoulders that enter every room before the rest of them does.
A slightly hunched posture that suggests long hours over a desk or workbench.
A tattoo visible at the wrist when the sleeve moves — geometric, old.
Heavy freckles across the nose and cheeks, present regardless of sun exposure.
A scar from a deep slash across the forearm, healed clean.
One eye that is slightly cloudy, the vision in it uncertain.
A very strong jaw that gives the lower face a squared, determined look.
A soft jaw that makes the face look younger and slightly less resolved than their bearing suggests.
A very wide forehead that dominates the face and gives them a slightly intellectual look.
A small, precise mouth that compresses to a line when they're deciding something.
A wide mouth with a rest position that defaults to slight amusement.
Scars across the knuckles of one hand that look like they came from a single incident.
Several piercing holes in the ears that are currently empty.
Several piercings that are currently in use — some clearly old additions, some recent.
Speaks with a slight accent that's almost completely suppressed but appears under stress.
Moves through crowds as if they have a path already planned — no hesitation, no course corrections.
A face so unremarkable that it's already fading from memory while you're still looking at it.
A scar that bisects the left eyebrow vertically, clean and old.
Skin with a warm undertone that makes them look slightly sun-warmed even indoors.
Skin with a cool undertone that makes them look slightly unwell in certain light.
Extremely large, expressive eyes that communicate before the face catches up.
Small, steady eyes that take in everything and give nothing back.
A slight permanent squint, as if everything they're looking at warrants skepticism.
A habitual slight frown that disappears when they actually concentrate.
A habitual slight smile that disappears when they're genuinely amused.
A neck that is unusually short, giving them an impression of being slightly larger than they are.
Hair worn extremely short for reasons that have nothing to do with practicality.
Hair worn extremely long for reasons that are clearly personal.
A right hand missing the little finger, old injury, adapted to.
A left hand missing the thumb, healed clean.
Extremely small for an adult — a height that would be unremarkable in a twelve-year-old.
Extremely large — not just tall but broadly built, taking up perceptibly more space than expected.
A very long torso relative to their legs, which changes how they look standing versus sitting.
Very long legs relative to their torso, which makes them look taller than they are until they sit.
An old wound visible on the collarbone — wide, well-healed, from something that should have been worse.
A ring worn on a chain at the neck rather than the hand.
Wears a single item of striking color against otherwise neutral clothing.
Wears entirely dark or neutral clothing regardless of context.
Wears remarkably practical clothing for their station.
Wears remarkably impractical clothing for their apparent trade.
Everything they wear is slightly too large, which gives them an air of carrying someone else's things.
Everything they wear fits precisely, clearly made for them or altered to their specifications.
Clothing visibly repaired in multiple places with different thread, worn as if it doesn't matter.
Clothing in excellent condition that is nonetheless clearly old — quality preserved over years.
Always carries something in the hands — a coin, a small stone, a pen — that moves between fingers constantly.
Keeps both hands visible at all times, which is noticeable once you notice it.
Often has one hand in a pocket or behind their back, which is noticeable once you notice it.
A very precise, brief, calibrated smile that communicates warmth without conveying information.
A very wide smile that takes over the whole face when it appears, which is rarely.
An extremely still face at rest — not cold, just at rest.
An extremely expressive face at rest — they don't realize how much they communicate when they're not speaking.
Heavily calloused feet visible when seated, suggesting years of walking without adequate footwear.
Very well-maintained boots that are clearly old friends rather than recent acquisitions.
Boots always slightly damp, regardless of conditions outside.
A brand scar on the inner forearm, old and faded.
A tattoo on the forearm that has been partially obscured by a later tattoo placed over it.
Old rope burns around the wrists, healed smooth, visible in low sleeves.
A face that reads differently depending on angle — gentle from one side, severe from the other.
A face that people consistently misremember as more attractive than it is, due to expression alone.
A face that people consistently remember as more plain than it is, due to expression alone.
Slight elevation in the left shoulder from an old injury never fully corrected.
A habit of blinking very slowly, once, when processing something important.
A habit of looking slightly to the left of whoever they're addressing, which reads as evasion but isn't.
Holds eye contact for slightly longer than is comfortable, without apparent awareness of this.
Breaks eye contact too quickly, which reads as evasion even when they're being direct.
Smells faintly of something specific — smoke, oil, herbs, animal — that identifies their trade.
Smells noticeably clean in a context where most people don't — it's the detail that sticks.
An extremely long and mobile face, capable of expressions most people's faces can't form.
A compact, precise face that doesn't waste movement.
Thick-framed spectacles that they wear pushed down the nose, always looking over rather than through.
Wire-framed spectacles, scratched, that sit slightly crooked.
Old pockmarks on the cheeks and chin from a childhood illness, faded but present.
A small circular scar on the back of the right hand — neat, deliberate-looking.
A mouth that doesn't quite close fully at rest — a slight habitual opening.
A jaw that clenches visibly when they're suppressing a response.
Cheeks that flush very visibly when they're excited, angry, or embarrassed — not always distinguishable.
A face that doesn't flush at all, which can make it hard to read.
A very prominent Adam's apple that moves constantly when they speak.
A throat scar from something long ago — the voice it left them with is lower and rougher than expected.
Carries visible evidence of a wound currently healing — wrapped hand, bandaged arm, careful movement.
A cluster of small round scars — not random, arranged — on the back of the hand.
Knuckles scarred from repetitive hard use — not fighting necessarily, just work.
A faded tattoo on the upper arm, too faded to make out what it was.
A fresh tattoo on the forearm, not yet fully healed, recently acquired.
Wears a small, specific item around their wrist — braided cord, narrow strap — that looks like it has been there for years.
Constantly adjusts their clothing — collar, cuffs, hem — as a reflex rather than a need.
Never adjusts their clothing, which results in a consistent slightly-disheveled look that's clearly accepted.
A face that compresses rather than crumbles under emotion — the expression gets smaller rather than larger.
A face that opens under emotion — eyes wider, expression more legible, as if the face has stopped managing itself.
Very dark circles under the eyes regardless of sleep — a structural feature, not a symptom.
The particular build of someone who was heavier once and carries the memory of it in their posture.
The particular bearing of someone who was more physically powerful once and hasn't entirely stopped expecting to be.
A stiffness in one wrist that becomes apparent when they lift or carry anything.
A slight reluctance in turning the head fully to the left, compensated for by turning the whole body.
Very large feet for their height, which they navigate confidently.
Very small feet for their height, which makes their footwear choices conspicuous.
A permanent slight redness to the skin of the nose and cheeks, suggesting outdoor work or something else entirely.
A very high brow that makes the face look attentive and slightly surprised at rest.
A very low brow that makes the face look intense and slightly suspicious at rest.
A full, grey-shot beard worn long and untrimmed, suggesting that trimming it would remove something.
A precisely trimmed beard that reads as a form of discipline.
A recently shaved face with a shadow that suggests the beard was recent and significant.
Skin with a texture — weathered, freckled, heavily lined — that tells the history of where they've been.
Skin with an unusual smoothness for their apparent age.
A narrow face that makes the eyes look further apart than they are.
A wide face that makes the eyes look closer together than they are.
The kind of muscular fitness that only comes from labor rather than exercise — different in character.
The kind of physical fitness that comes from deliberate training — visible even under clothing.
A slight cough that recurs throughout conversation and which they've stopped noticing.
A habit of clearing the throat before any statement they consider important.
Eyes that appear to be different colors depending on the light, landing somewhere between two shades.
A deeply lined face that reads as experience rather than age.
A smooth face that reads as guarded — the lines haven't been allowed to form.
An extremely upright bearing that reads as military until you realize it's just habit.
An extremely relaxed bearing that reads as confidence until you notice the eyes aren't relaxed at all.
A face that looks exactly like someone the party has met before. It isn't them.
A face with no single striking feature — and yet, when they leave, you find you can describe it in complete detail.
A small gap between the front teeth that affects how certain sounds land.
Lips chapped and dry regardless of climate.
An extremely mobile face when listening — responding to every nuance of what's being said.
A completely still face when listening — like a statue made from someone attentive.
A prominent nose that anchors the face and makes every emotion slightly more dramatic.
A very small nose that the rest of the face seems to have arranged itself around.
A broad, flat nose that dominates the lower face.
A hooked nose that, combined with the eyes, gives them a raptorial quality.
A slightly upturned nose that gives the face a perpetually inquiring expression.
An old frostbite scar on the tips of two fingers, white and smooth.
A thin horizontal scar across the throat, perfectly placed, old and healed.
A thick diagonal scar across the collarbone visible above any neckline.
Redness across the backs of the hands and wrists from years of exposure to something caustic.
Wears their hair pulled completely back, which removes it as a variable and makes the face everything.
Wears their hair loose in a way that is neither styled nor unstyled — just present.
A strong smell of a specific herbal preparation that they use medicinally without comment.
Notably dry skin across the hands and knuckles that cracks in cold weather.
Notably soft hands for someone who appears to do physical work.
A single long scar running diagonally across the back of one hand from wrist to knuckle.
A birthmark at the hairline, usually hidden, occasionally visible.
Visible rope-burn scars around both wrists, healed to a smooth band.
The rolling gait of someone who spent years at sea even though they're now landlocked.
A particular stillness in their hands when they speak — they gesture with their voice, not their body.
Hands in constant motion — not nervous movement, but expressive, communicative.
A slight tilt of the head to the right at rest — so habitual it's stopped seeming like a choice.
A noticeable smell of specific wood — oak, cedar, pine — from their work.
A permanent smudge or stain under one eye, like a bruise, that is neither.
Three small matching tattoos on the inside of the right wrist — old, neat, professional work.
A missing tooth at the side of the mouth, visible when they smile widely.
A chipped front tooth that gives their speech a slight whistle on certain sounds.
A face with an upper lip significantly more prominent than the lower.
A face with a lower lip significantly more prominent than the upper.
Old acne scarring across the cheeks and jaw, present but faded.
Skin so weathered that age is genuinely impossible to determine — could be anywhere across a thirty-year range.
Hands aged well beyond the rest of them — the hands of someone thirty years older.
Hands that look younger than the rest of them — soft, unmarked, or carefully maintained.
A wide, flat chest that makes posture and bearing look more neutral than they are.
A slight forward lean when standing still, like they're perpetually on the edge of doing something.
A very slight speech impediment — a stutter that has been mostly trained away but reappears under stress.
An extremely clear, projecting voice that sounds trained even in casual conversation.
A very quiet voice that requires people to lean in and then to remember to lean back.
A voice that carries without effort — they don't raise it, but it travels.
A gravelly voice that sounds like something happened to it and it recovered better than expected.
A smooth voice that sounds like nothing has ever happened to it at all.
A very young-sounding voice in an older face.
A very old-sounding voice in a younger face.
A face that compresses a smile into the eyes instead of the mouth, so they smile without appearing to.
A face that smiles with the mouth without reaching the eyes, not insincerely — the two systems just work separately.
An old bite scar on the forearm — human, not animal.
A scar on the chin from something blunt and recent, still pink.
Very broad, flat feet that make their stance wide and stable.
A strong, unusual smell — not unpleasant — that takes a moment to identify.
No smell at all, which becomes noticeable in a crowd.
Old knife scar across the ribs, visible in certain clothing.
A very short torso relative to their height — mostly leg — which makes seated posture look different.
An unusual amount of gray in their eyebrows compared to the rest of their hair.
Eyebrows that grow thick and untamed regardless of anything done about them.
Eyebrows that are naturally thin, giving the face a slightly surprised look at rest.
A pattern of old, small burns on one forearm from work with fire or heat.
A specific catch in their breath before any statement that might be difficult — not a stutter, a bracing.
Heavy-lidded eyes that give them a permanently sleepy look unrelated to their actual state.
Wide-open eyes that give them a permanently alert look unrelated to their actual state.
A fading bruise on the cheekbone, greenish, a week old.
A healing split lip, not yet fully closed.
A healing cut above the eye, recently stitched.
A single finger that bends at an unusual angle from a break that healed wrong.
Two fingers that no longer fully straighten, fused slightly at an angle.
A thumb that will no longer bend at the first joint — old injury, fully adapted to.
A chronic redness to the eyes that reads as either emotion or irritation and is neither.
Very clear, very white eyes — the whites unusually bright.
The particular posture of someone who has carried heavy things over long distances for most of their life.
The particular posture of someone who has sat over a writing surface for most of their life.
The particular posture of someone who has stood behind a counter for most of their life.
A significant scar on the lower lip, healed white.
An absurd amount of physical charisma — they move well, and the room notices without knowing why.
A complete absence of physical self-consciousness, as though the body is simply a tool and the observation of it irrelevant.
Extremely visible physical self-consciousness — they're aware of being looked at and it affects everything.
A very old arrow wound on the upper arm, partially visible as a round, puckered scar.
A shrapnel scar scattered across the back of one hand — multiple small raised marks.
A puncture scar on the shoulder, front and back — something went through.
A stiff right knee that gives the leg a slightly straight-legged quality when they walk.
A stiff left ankle, adapted to over years, noticeable only when they're tired.
A habit of closing their eyes briefly when accessing a memory, as though looking inward.
A habit of looking at the ceiling briefly before answering a complex question.
A habit of looking at the floor briefly before delivering bad news.
Skin that holds color visibly — bruises, blushes, and pressure marks appear fast and stay.
Skin that shows nothing, which makes injury and emotion both harder to read.
A face that photographs perfectly, which means it reads as slightly artificial in person.
A face that photographs poorly but reads as immediately trustworthy in person.
A pair of old matching scars on the inside of the wrists, thin and long, old enough to have fully faded.
A habit of keeping their chin down, which makes eye contact upward and slightly guarded.
A habit of keeping their chin up, which makes eye contact downward and slightly imposing.
Extremely wide-set eyes that give the face a panoramic, slightly watchful quality.
Extremely close-set eyes that give the face an intense, slightly focused quality.
A very square skull that makes the head look like it was cut from something.
A very round skull that gives the head a slightly childlike quality.
Hair that is clearly dyed — not trying to hide this, just maintaining it.
Hair that has been bleached by sun to several different shades at once.
A small but notable limp from a foot injury that they've compensated for so well it reads as a walk style.
A significant limp from a leg injury that they've accepted rather than concealed.
An old, deep cut on the palm that left a thick scar line they trace unconsciously when thinking.
A cluster of old splinter scars in the thumb and index finger from years of fine woodwork.
A brand mark on the upper arm, partially obscured by a tattoo over it.
Extremely long eyelashes that make every blink look deliberate.
Extremely sparse eyelashes that make the eyes look more exposed.
Cheekbones so prominent they cast visible shadows in side light.
Cheekbones barely visible under soft features.
A very strong, prominent brow ridge that gives the face an intense, slightly prehistoric quality.
A very recessed brow that makes the face look open and slightly vulnerable.
A long scar on the inside of the left forearm, clearly self-administered at some point.
The particular weight and economy of movement of someone who has kept themselves in condition under difficult circumstances.
The slight uncertainty of movement of someone recovering from something significant.
A deeply lined forehead that suggests either great age or great worry.
A smooth forehead that suggests either good fortune or a remarkable capacity to stop worrying.
A deep crease between the brows that appears when they're concentrating and doesn't fully relax.
Smile lines so deep they're present even when the face is at rest.
Frown lines only, with no corresponding smile lines — their face remembers one more than the other.
Wears a specific piece of jewelry that is clearly out of place with everything else they own.
Wears no jewelry at all, not as an aesthetic choice but as if the category doesn't apply to them.
Wears a single ring on an unusual finger — not the standard choices.
A distinctive smell of a specific smoke — pipe, incense, wood — that follows them.
A distinctive smell of a specific food preparation that follows them from their trade.
A distinctive smell of specific animals that follows them from their work.
A distinctive smell of metal — forge work, old weapons — that follows them.
A distinctive smell of earth and stone that follows them from underground work.
A distinctive smell of the sea — salt, tar — that stays long after they've left it.
A distinctive smell of a specific medicinal compound.
A distinctive smell of old paper and leather.
A distinctive smell of fresh-cut wood.
A distinctive smell of wool, lanolin, and damp.
An elongated face with features that seem stretched — long chin, long nose, deep-set eyes.
A compressed face with features that seem pushed together — features close, distances short.
An extremely symmetrical face that reads as almost artificial.
An extremely asymmetrical face that reads as more expressive than it is.
The skin of someone who has been very sick and come back from it — thin, careful, not quite finished recovering.
The skin of someone who has been very well for a very long time — healthy to the point of seeming slightly unreal.
A face dominated by one feature — nose, eyes, jaw — that the rest organizes around.
A face without a dominant feature, where everything is average and the combination is striking.
A single very visible vein at the temple that pulses when they're under stress.
Extremely pale lips that make the face look more severe than the expression warrants.
Very dark lips that make the face read as more dramatic than the person usually intends.
Very full lips that give the rest of the face's neutral expressions a perpetual softness.
Very thin lips that give the rest of the face's expressions a precise, economical quality.
A habit of running a hand over the back of the neck when uncertain.
A habit of pressing the lips together briefly before delivering any statement of consequence.
A habit of tucking their chin before asking a difficult question.
A habit of stretching the neck slightly to the left when thinking.
A habit of tapping the thumb against the index finger when processing something.
A habit of making a brief, small sound — not quite a word — when they've understood something.
A habit of briefly closing the eyes when they're deciding something — one second, no more.
An old bite wound on the forearm from a large animal, healed and faded.
A puncture scar in the left palm, small and neat, healed completely.
A crosshatch of very old scars on one palm, from something repetitive and long past.
A tattoo that has been partially removed — the ghost of it is still present beneath the skin.
A tattoo that covers a scar — you can tell because the ink is slightly raised.
Wears their sleeves pushed up in a way that suggests they always have — habitual, unconscious.
Wears their sleeves down in all circumstances, which after a while becomes conspicuous.
A chin that recedes slightly, which gives the profile a soft edge the full face doesn't suggest.
A chin that projects, which gives the profile a determined edge the full face doesn't always suggest.
A cleft chin that makes the face symmetrical in a specific, old-fashioned way.
Very dark circles beneath the eyes that are not from sleeplessness but from structure.
No visible circles at all, which makes the face look slightly more rested than the person is.
A face that tans extremely easily and is always two shades darker than expected for the season.
A face that doesn't tan at all and burns instead — the scars of this are faded into the texture.
A very prominent lower jaw that makes the profile the more powerful angle.
A very soft lower jaw that makes the profile the more uncertain angle.
A habit of dropping to a slight crouch when examining something, which looks very natural and slightly animalistic.
A habit of coming to a full stop when thinking — no movement at all, not even small adjustments.
A habit of looking at hands when speaking about something difficult.
A habit of using objects nearby — coins, pen, edge of the table — to organize thoughts while speaking.
Very pale gums that make the teeth look starker and the smile colder than intended.
Naturally very red gums that give the smile unusual warmth.
Teeth slightly larger than the mouth, which creates a perpetual impression that they could bite harder than expected.
Teeth slightly smaller than the mouth, which creates a perpetual impression of a gap between word and intent.
A specific difficulty with one consonant that they've spent years reducing and which reappears under pressure.
A rolling, almost musical quality to their speech regardless of language or content.
A flat, declarative quality to their speech regardless of language or content.
An unusual amount of physical grace — they move well in a way that requires no thought.
An unusual amount of physical awkwardness — they move as though they and their body have an ongoing negotiation.
A slightly animal quality to the way they move through spaces — all peripheral awareness and energy conservation.
A slightly mechanical quality to the way they move — very consistent, very calibrated, very little wasted.
A slight droop to one eyelid that makes one eye seem more closed than the other at rest.
Eyes that are naturally wide, giving them an expression of perpetual openness.
Eyes that are naturally narrow, giving them an expression of perpetual evaluation.
A very faint accent overlaid on a clearly trained speech pattern — the training was deliberate and almost successful.
A very clear accent that they haven't bothered to reduce — it's part of how they present themselves.
No accent at all, which to people who know the region is itself the tell.
Ears that have been cauliflowered by old impact — they've taken hits to the head and it shows.
Ears with multiple piercings in various states of healing and age.
Ears with a single piercing that hasn't been used in a long time.
Ears with a small notch in one lobe — something caught or cut it long ago.
A single very large and distinctive scar that the body has been built around — everything about how they dress and move relates to it.
A body with no visible scars at all, which for someone of their apparent history reads as strange.
A body with many small scars from ordinary life — minor wounds accumulated over years of active existence.
The eyes of someone who has seen a great deal and decided to continue seeing things anyway.
The eyes of someone who has made a deliberate decision about what to look at and what not to.
The eyes of someone who is still deciding.
The posture of someone who has made peace with everything except the current situation.
The posture of someone who has not yet made peace with anything but is working on it.
The face of someone who has had the exact same conversation many times before and is prepared to have it again.
A very broad, slightly flat nose with wide nostrils that gives the face an open quality.
Very deep-set eyes above prominent cheekbones, so that the eyes are almost in shadow.
An extremely smooth brow that gives the face a youthful look regardless of other features.
Ears that lie very flat against the head, almost disappearing into the face.
A pronounced overbite that makes the profile more dramatic than the front view.
A pronounced underbite that gives the face a forward quality, like they're already leaning in.
Teeth that are completely even and white in a face that otherwise looks like it's had a hard life.
Teeth that are completely uneven in a face that otherwise looks very composed.
A prominent Adam's apple that bobs when they're nervous.
A very strong neck that makes the head look well-supported even in relaxed posture.
A very thin neck that makes the head look precarious.
A strong jaw line that becomes visible when they're tense.
A jaw line that softens rather than tenses under pressure.
A very long chin that makes the face look thoughtful from below.
A very short chin that makes the face compact and close-featured.
Old bruise discoloration around one eye that never fully healed.
A permanent slight redness across the nose bridge from cold or exposure.
A nose that has been reset at least twice — the most recent setting is better than the last but not perfect.
A small notch in the tip of the nose from old damage, subtle but there.
A bump at the bridge of the nose from an old break that healed with character.
A distinctive hand gesture they make when making a point — specific, unconscious, always the same.
A distinctive hand gesture they make when uncertain — different from the above, unconscious, always the same.
A distinctive hand gesture they make when dismissing something — brief, final, communicative.
An extremely firm handshake — not aggressive, just absolute.
An extremely light handshake — not limp, just reserved.
A two-handed handshake that is either warmth or control depending on how you receive it.
Very long arms relative to the torso — they reach things easily that they shouldn't be able to.
Very short arms relative to the torso — they gesture close to their body as a result.
A face that is younger in repose and older when animated.
A face that is older in repose and younger when animated.
A very particular way of sitting — the body arranged deliberately, as though being painted.
A very particular way of sitting — entirely functional, nothing arranged for effect.
A way of entering rooms — pausing at the threshold, taking inventory, then proceeding.
A way of entering rooms — going directly to where they're going, no pause.
A way of entering rooms — always taking the seat with the best sightline before sitting.
A way of sitting that keeps the back unsupported, even in chairs with backs.
A way of sitting that uses every available support — as settled as the furniture allows.
A way of standing that takes up more space than their size would suggest.
A way of standing that takes up less space than their size would suggest — compressed, careful, contained.
Hands that are always cold, noticeably so when they shake hands or pass something.
Hands that are always warm, noticeably so — a minor comfort to people who are cold.
A very deliberate way of moving objects — setting them down carefully, picking them up completely.
A very careless way of moving objects — not destructively, just loosely.
A habit of keeping something in the hand when standing — a cup, a pen, a document.
A habit of keeping nothing in the hands, both sides always empty and visible.
A very particular smell of a specific wood-smoke variety — pine, oak, peat — that seems to follow them everywhere.
A very particular smell of a specific food — bread, spice, oil — from long work around it.
A very particular smell of a specific chemical — tallow, pitch, turpentine — from their trade.
An absence of any identifying smell that, in a trade that would usually produce one, is itself information.
The slightly elevated posture of someone who was always the shortest person in the room and adapted.
The slightly reduced posture of someone who was always the tallest person in the room and adapted.
A habit of adjusting their clothing before any important statement.
A habit of adjusting someone else's clothing if it's visibly wrong — collar, lapel, thread — without comment.
A habit of noticing everyone's footwear first.
A habit of noticing everyone's hands first.
A habit of noticing everyone's exits first.
A very deliberate slowness in their movements that reads as either calm or threat depending on context.
A very deliberate quickness in their movements that reads as either efficiency or urgency depending on context.
An extremely controlled face that gives nothing away until it does, and then gives everything.
An extremely readable face that gives everything away continuously and seems untroubled by this.
A face that people trust immediately and usually correctly.
A face that people distrust immediately and usually incorrectly.
A face that people find difficult to read, not because it's guarded but because its signals are unusual.
A face that people find easy to read but often misread.
Eyes that track movement constantly — not paranoid, just extremely aware of where things are.
Eyes that focus on one thing at a time, completely, which makes the subject of their attention feel very attended to.
Eyes that look through things rather than at them, which can be unsettling when it's you they're looking through.
Eyes that meet every gaze like an equal regardless of the power dynamics involved.
Eyes that calibrate their gaze precisely to the relationship — less for subordinates, more for equals, something specific for authority.
A face that has clearly been in good condition and has fallen slightly — not abandoned, just dealt with.
A face that has clearly been in poor condition and has risen slightly — not quite finished, but clearly trying.
A face that reads as simply uninterested in what it looks like, which paradoxically makes it more interesting.
A face that reads as very interested in what it looks like, which is not vanity but craft.
The particular quiet stillness of someone who learned not to react as a survival mechanism.
The particular expressiveness of someone who decided early on to let everything show.
A scar at the corner of the eye — small, old, from something too close.
A scar at the hairline on the forehead, narrow, healed to a white line.
A scar on the upper lip, vertical, healed smooth but visible.
A scar on the jaw, angled, old, from a blade.
A scar on the lower lid, thin, from something sharp.
The look of someone who was young very recently and has been asked to grow up fast.
The look of someone who was old very recently and has been asked to start over.
A body that looks stronger than it is — the posture is excellent.
A body that looks weaker than it is — the clothing is loose.
A body that is exactly as strong as it looks — the appearance and the reality coincide perfectly.
The particular economy of movement of someone who spent years doing a precise, repetitive physical task.
The particular breadth of movement of someone who spent years doing a large, irregular physical task.
A head that is carried very precisely level — no tilt, no drop, like something balanced there.
A head that is carried with a slight forward cant — thinking, always slightly ahead.
A head that is carried slightly back — evaluating, always slightly behind.
A body with excellent basic posture and careful management of a specific injury.
A body with poor posture and no apparent awareness of it.
A body with deliberately poor posture that conceals what the actual bearing is.
The bearing of someone who was trained to present themselves well and has forgotten they're doing it.
The bearing of someone who was trained to present themselves well and does it very deliberately.
A face that changes completely between when it's watched and when it thinks it isn't.
A face that is exactly the same whether watched or not.
A face that is more itself when it thinks it isn't watched, which is when it becomes most worth watching.
The particular stillness of someone who has decided, finally, what they are and what they're not.
Something about them that you can't name — a presence, a quality, an absence of something that should be there — that makes you want to look again.
